relationship_to_restored_text: Interpretive contemplative layer aligned by verse. This does not replace the source-audited restored translation.

verses.mystical_translation: Eliphaz began the first human answer to Job’s pain.
He could not hold back from speaking.
He remembered Job as one who strengthened others.
Job’s words had once lifted stumbling people.
But now suffering had touched Job himself.
Eliphaz appealed to Job’s fear of the Divine and integrity.
He offered the neat rule that the innocent do not perish.
He framed suffering as harvest from seeds of harm.
He imagined divine breath consuming the guilty.
Even lion-strength, he said, is broken.
Power without prey scatters.
Then Eliphaz turned to a secret whisper he had received.
The vision came in the deep thoughts of night.
Dread shook him.
A spirit passed before his face.
A form stood beyond recognition, and a voice emerged from silence.
The voice asked whether any mortal can be purer than the Divine.
Even heavenly servants were not beyond error in this vision.
How fragile, then, are humans dwelling in clay and dust.
Morning to evening, mortality crushes them.
Their tent-cord is pulled, and they die without wisdom.
